0.q: SOD activity in transgenic lines was significantly higher than WT plants... CAT activity in transgenic plants was significantly higher than that in WT plants... suggesting that ROS-scavenging capacity may be enhanced in the transgenic plants because of elevated SOD and CAT activity.

3.q: A yeast two‐hybrid screen revealed that LbRZF1 interacted with LbCATALASE2 (LbCAT2) and the transcription factor LbMYB113, leading to their stabilization.

4.q: a yeast two-hybrid, bimolecular fluorescence complementation and GST pull-down assays indicated that the hypothetical protein Lb2G12567 interacts with LbSAD2.

5.q: Y2H library screening revealed a strong interaction between LbFLZ13 and LbNAC55. Strong yellow fluorescence signals were observed in the nucleus through BiFC. In the Co-IP assay, LbNAC55-GFP and LbFLZ13-HA proteins were successfully immunoprecipitated when coexpressed. GST pull-down also proves the interaction.

8.q: LbHDZ14 negatively regulates salt gland density and salt tolerance by directly binding to the promoter of LbGDSL, a positive regulator of salt gland development.

9.q: LbYAB1 negatively regulates salt gland development and salt tolerance by repressing the expression of SUPER SENSITIVE TO ABA AND DROUGHT 2 (LbSAD2)

10.q: Lb2G12077 can bind to the LbSAD2 promoter to inhibit the transcription of LbSAD2 verified by a yeast one-hybrid, electrophoretic mobility shift and dual-luciferase reporter assay.

11.q: The expression of several genes [LbTTG1, LbCPC, and LbGL2] related to salt gland development was significantly upregulated in LbMAPK2 knockdown lines.

12.q: The expression of several genes [LbTTG1, LbCPC, and LbGL2] related to salt gland development was significantly upregulated in LbMAPK2 knockdown lines.

13.q: The expression of several genes [LbTTG1, LbCPC, and LbGL2] related to salt gland development was significantly upregulated in LbMAPK2 knockdown lines.

14.q: LbMUSTANG3 (LbMUG3) was identified to bind to the LbSTI promoter and inhibit the transcription of LbSTI using a yeast one-hybrid and dual-luciferase reporter assay.

15.q: LbRZF1 interacted with LbCATALASE2 (LbCAT2) and the transcription factor LbMYB113, leading to their stabilization.

16.q: LbRZF1 interacted with LbCATALASE2 (LbCAT2) and the transcription factor LbMYB113, leading to their stabilization.

17.q: the expression of LbEGL3 (ENHANCER OF GL3) was significantly downregulated [in LbMAPK2 knockdown lines].

18.q: LbHDZ14...negatively regulates salt gland density and salt tolerance by directly binding to the promoter of LbGDSL, a positive regulator of salt gland development.

19.q: We also identified the transcription factor LbMYB48 as an upstream regulator of LbRZF1 transcription.

20.q: LbbZIP52 can specifically bind to the ABRE element in the LbHKT1;1 promoter and regulate the expression of the LbHKT1;1 gene and is involved in the negative regulation of the salt secretion capacity of L. bicolor.

21.q: the upstream regulatory transcription factor LbMUSTANG3 (LbMUG3) was identified to bind to the LbSTI promoter and inhibit the transcription of LbSTI using a yeast one-hybrid and dual-luciferase reporter assay.

22.q: The heterologous over-expression of LbMYB48 in Arabidopsis thaliana improves salt tolerance of plants by stabilizing ion and osmotic balance and is likely to be involved in the abscisic acid signaling pathway.

23.q: The LbYAB1–LbKNAT7 interaction enhanced the transcriptional repression of LbSAD2, thus regulating salt gland development. Our work places LbYAB1 upstream of LbSAD2 in salt gland development.

24.q: Multiple assays (a yeast two-hybrid, split-luciferase complementation and GST pull-down) supported the interaction of LbYAB1 with LbKNAT7.

25.q: Multiple assays (a yeast two-hybrid, split-luciferase complementation and GST pull-down) supported the interaction of LbYAB1 with LbKNAT7 (Homeobox protein knotted-1-like 7)
